etymology.description: 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") + 羊(羊) (yáng, "sheep") -> dog and sheep associated with good fortune
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") and 羊(羊) (yáng, "sheep"), symbolizing animals that were traditionally seen as bringing good fortune. The combination reflects the belief that possessing such animals would bring luck and prosperity.

examples.chinese: 这是我最幸运的一天(這是我最幸運的一天)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì wǒ zuì xìngyùn de yī tiān.

examples.chinese: 幸运的是(幸運的是)，我们赶上了最后一班车(我們趕上了最後一班車)。
examples.pinyin: Xìngyùn de shì, wǒmen gǎn shàng le zuìhòu yī bān chē.
examples.english: Fortunately, we caught the last bus.

examples.chinese: 他感到自己很幸运(他感到自己很幸運)，因为有一个支持他的家庭(因為有一個支持他的家庭)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào zìjǐ hěn xìngyùn, yīnwèi yǒu yīgè zhīchí tā de jiātíng.
examples.english: He feels fortunate because he has a supportive family.
